name: Ensure AWS Inspector is configured for EC2 Instances
body:
### Control intent

This control verifies that **aWS Inspector is configured for EC2 Instances** in AWS.

### Signal reviewed

The assessment checks for aWS Inspector is configured for EC2 Instances and highlights environments where security telemetry or alerting coverage is incomplete.

### Why it is operationally important

Security teams need reliable telemetry before an incident starts. Missing audit logs, weak retention, or absent alerts can make investigation, evidence collection, and response timelines materially worse.
description: AWS Inspector is configured for EC2 Instances
remediationDescription:
## AWS remediation

### Console
1. Open Amazon Inspector.
2. Enable scanning for EC2 resources.
3. Verify coverage for all required regions/accounts.

### AWS CLI
~~~bash
aws inspector2 enable --resource-types EC2
aws inspector2 list-coverage --filter-criteria '{}'
~~~

### References
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/inspector/latest/user/inspector_introduction.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/inspector2/enable.html
